projectDescription: An Arduino library for the XPT2046 resistive touchscreen controller chip found on many low-cost TFT displays, providing SPI-based touch detection, coordinate reading, and interrupt support.
keyFeatures: SPI-based communication with XPT2046 touch controller at 2 MHz
Touch detection via touched() and tirqTouched() methods
Coordinate reading via getPoint() (returns TS_Point) and readData()
Optional interrupt pin (T_IRQ) support for efficient polling without SPI calls when not touched
Configurable screen rotation (0–3) to match TFT display orientation
Noise filtering using best-two-of-three averaging algorithm
Support for alternate SPI ports (e.g., SPI1) and Teensy FlexIO SPI
Works on Arduino, Teensy, ESP8266, ESP32, and other Arduino-compatible boards
publicApiSurface: bool begin(SPIClass &wspi = SPI) — initialize with optional SPI port
bool begin(FlexIOSPI &wflexspi) — initialize with FlexIO SPI (Teensy)
bool touched() — returns true if screen is currently touched
bool tirqTouched() — fast IRQ-based touch check without SPI
TS_Point getPoint() — returns TS_Point with x, y, z coordinates
void readData(uint16_t *x, uint16_t *y, uint8_t *z) — reads coordinates into pointers
bool bufferEmpty() — returns true if recent data is stale
uint8_t bufferSize() — returns internal buffer size (always 1)
